meta.title: Deep Space Echo
meta.opening:
You are Federation Investigator K-09. Seventy-two hours ago, Deep Space Research Station Orion-7 lost all contact with ground control. Your mission: board the station, investigate, come back alive.

The airlock door seals slowly behind you. Inside the station, the emergency lights pulse a dim red —

What do you do?

locations.name: Airlock
locations.description: The main entrance to Orion-7. Two heavy airlock doors, inner and outer, with a yellowed safety manual taped to the wall. A dark drag-mark stretches along the floor toward the main corridor.

locations.name: Main Corridor
locations.description: The central passage connecting all functional compartments. The emergency lights flicker intermittently; the gravity system is unstable, with occasional brief weightlessness.

locations.name: Control Room
locations.description: The brain of the station. The holographic console is still active, Station Chief Mira's terminal is still lit, and the last log entry is frozen at 72 hours ago.

locations.name: Hydroponics Bay
locations.description: Originally used for oxygen and food production. Now vines grow abnormally, covering most of the walls. A cloying, sweet odor of rot hangs in the air.

locations.name: Engine Room
locations.description: The reactor control area. A sustained low-frequency hum fills the space, and unexplained dragging sounds occasionally come from the ventilation ducts. Slightly warmer than other areas.

locations.name: Research Lab
locations.description: A biological-sample laboratory. Most of the glass incubation chambers are shattered. A scattered experiment notebook mentions 'the consciousness-diffusion phenomenon of specimen M-7'.

characters.name: Mira Chen
characters.description: Chief of Orion-7, age 42, former deep-space biologist. Her final log reveals she had ethical doubts about the research on 'specimen M-7' and planned to report it to headquarters.

characters.name: Raj Patel
characters.description: Chief engineer. Logs repeatedly mention anomalies in the engine room. His quarters are locked from the inside, and faint tapping sounds come from within.

characters.name: Eva
characters.description: The station's AI assistant. Still operating, but her answers now contain illogical pauses. She falls silent on certain questions (especially about specimen M-7).

characters.name: Unknown Entity
characters.description: A presence in the ventilation shafts. No stable form — appears to have emerged from the diffusion of specimen M-7. Sensitive to light and sound.

factions.name: Federation Bureau of Investigation
factions.description: The player's organization. Wants to uncover the truth, but may also suppress information. The mission comes with strict time limits.

factions.name: Helios Biotech Group
factions.description: Orion-7's funding sponsor. Has a strong interest in the commercial value of specimen M-7 and may have dispatched its own recovery team.

items.name: Mira's ID Card
items.description: ID card picked up in the control room. Dead but with an intact chip. Unlocks the research lab and Mira's personal terminal.

items.name: Tactical Flashlight
items.description: The player's starting equipment. Its high-intensity beam repels the unknown entity, but battery life is limited.

items.name: Experiment Notebook
items.description: A handwritten journal. The last page reads: 'M-7 is starting to respond to thought. This isn't right.'

rules.name: Time Pressure
rules.description: Every 10 in-game turns, the station's life-support system drops by one level. After level 4, the air runs out and the game ends.

rules.name: Light Defense
rules.description: The unknown entity retreats in bright light. The flashlight can briefly drive it off, but drains battery.

rules.name: Three Truths
rules.description: The game has three possible endings: (1) bring the specimen back to the Federation, (2) destroy the specimen, (3) make a deal with Helios. The player's key choices determine which ending plays out.
